# State Management Checklist: Non-Stopping Reviewability Markers

**Purpose**: Validate PR marker state, resume, workflow evidence, and emission drift requirements before task generation
**Created**: 2026-06-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are persisted marker-plan requirements defined for both `autopilot-state.json` and workflow evidence without making `tasks.md` authoritative? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the spec define all required persisted marker fields needed for planning, implementation checkpoints, and PR emission mapping? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK003 Does the marker-plan fingerprint include every artifact that can change marker scope, including plan-declared files/tests as well as spec, tasks, reviewability evidence, and hazard route? [Resolved, Spec §FR-014, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK004 Are resume requirements explicit about when existing marker implementation and emission evidence is preserved, cleared, or replaced after marker-plan recomputation? [Resolved, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK005 Are workflow evidence fields for reviewability sizing, marker decisions, and PR emission specified strongly enough to prevent drift from `autopilot-state.json`? [Resolved, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Marker Evidence]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is the authoritative ordering rule clear for persisted `markers[]` and one-based `review_order`? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK007 Is `marker_split` defined as a final-backstop proceed outcome when the full diff is size-blocked and the marker plan is current and valid? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the status lifecycle for `pr_marker_plan` clear enough for resume and final emission to distinguish planned, checkpointing, emission-ready, emitted, collapsed, stale, and invalid states? [Clarity, Plan §Data Model]
- [ ] CHK009 Are workflow evidence and state evidence required to use the same marker IDs, review order, fingerprints, and warning objects rather than parallel prose summaries? [Resolved, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Marker Evidence]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Are state staleness rules consistent with correctness stops for stale fingerprints and malformed marker plans? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK011 Are marker IDs consistent across Foundation, user-story, subdivided story, and hazard-collapsed marker kinds? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK012 Is the relationship between subdivided `us<N>-part<M>` markers and the original parent `us<N>` marker specified so PR emission cannot double-emit or skip a story? [Resolved, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §PR Marker]
- [ ] CHK013 Are hazard-collapse requirements consistent between implementation checkpoint ordering and one-PR emission mapping? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK014 Are missing, malformed, stale, or fingerprint-mismatched marker plans covered as final-emission correctness stops? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK015 Are task reviewability `status=block` results covered as marker input only when parseable, current, and size-only? [Coverage, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK016 Are safe-subdivision requirements defined for dependency boundaries, declared files/tests, shared mutation, hazard signals, and order preservation? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK017 Are Polish folding target rules specific enough to decide where cleanup tasks go without creating cleanup-only markers or changing review order? [Resolved, Spec §FR-005, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK018 Do success criteria measure marker persistence, ordering, emission behavior, hazard collapse, and correctness stops with deterministic fixtures? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001-SC-007]
- [ ] CHK019 Do verification requirements cover both deterministic script behavior and guidance-level functional evals for non-stopping reviewability state? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK020 Are review packet requirements tied back to marker state fields so warnings and review order are generated from durable evidence? [Traceability, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]

## Notes

- Items carrying gap markers require spec.md and/or plan.md remediation before G4 can pass.

## Re-Run Verification

- [ ] CHK021 Does the updated spec require `source_fingerprint` coverage for spec, plan-declared scope, tasks, reviewability evidence, and hazard route? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK022 Does the updated plan define workflow evidence parity with `autopilot-state.json` for marker IDs, review order, fingerprints, warnings, checkpoints, and emission mapping? [Consistency, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK023 Are resume preservation and reset rules deterministic at marker scope? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK024 Does the contract schema require the new state fields needed for plan-scope fingerprints, subdivision parentage, and Polish fold rationale? [Traceability, Contract §pr-marker-plan.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK025 Are subdivided user-story markers defined as replacing the parent marker for scoped emission? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK026 Are Polish folding target rules specific enough to prevent cleanup-only markers and state/workflow drift? [Coverage, Spec §FR-005]
